question_text: In an experiment, a wave with frequency 10 Hz and wavelength 2 m travels through medium X. The same wave then enters medium Y where its speed increases to 30 m/s. Comparing the two media, which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The wavelength in medium Y is 2 m and the frequency is 15 Hz

options.label: B
options.text: The wavelength in medium Y is 3 m and the frequency remains 10 Hz

options.label: C
options.text: Both wavelength and frequency increase in medium Y

options.label: D
options.text: The wave speed in medium X is 20 m/s and wavelength doubles in medium Y

explanation: In medium X: v = f × λ = 10 × 2 = 20 m/s. When entering medium Y, frequency stays constant at 10 Hz (source-dependent). With v = 30 m/s and f = 10 Hz, the new wavelength is λ = v/f = 30/10 = 3 m. Only frequency determined by the source remains constant; wavelength and speed change with the medium.
